avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The winds will redistribute recent snow deposits with new localised pockets of unstable windslab developing on North-West through North-East to East aspects above 900 metres. Steep wind sheltered locations such as coire rims and gully tops will be most affected particularly where accumulations develop over old snow. Elsewhere, areas exposed to the winds will be very icy. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Cold overnight but freezing level lifting to above the summits during the day. Dry throughout. Winds strengthening from the South, veering to the South-South-West for most of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Snowfall overnight was followed by a cold clear day. Winds were light and variable.
ObservedSnowStability: There was a thin covering of snow on most aspects above 500 metres. Most of this was cosmetic but there were isolated unstable deposits found mostly on North-East through South-East to Southerly aspects above 800 metres. These areas were restricted to wind sheltered features such as gully tops, corrie rims and steep convex slopes. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Good stability in most locations.  There may be some redistribution of snow on to very steep North West through North to North East aspects above 1000m where some localised moderately bonded windslab is possible. Where exposed, the old snowpack will remain hard or firm. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: No precipitation is forecast. Winds will strengthen and become moderate to strong Southerly. Generally cold but milder later.
Observed Weather Influences: Overnight snow showers on North-West winds then dry, bright and cold. Winds eased to light variable during the day.
ObservedSnowStability: Good stability in most locations. Accumulations of localised soft windslab lie on some very steep East to South aspects above 950m. Weak warmth from the sun brought some consolidation to all new snow by late morning. There is a general covering of thin fresh snow in most places above 750m. The limited old snowpack is refrozen, hard and well stabilised. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will remain generally well bonded and stable. Isolated and shallow areas of windslab will be present mainly on North-West through North to East aspects above 900 metres; these should be generally avoidable. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It will remain dry with gale force South-South-Westerly winds. The freezing level will rise above the summits during the morning.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a dry day with moderate South-South-Westerly winds, increasing in the afternoon. The freezing level rose above the summits in the afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: The older snowpack is very firm with good stability. Fresh snow accumulations from yesterday are shallow and insignificant, thawing quickly in the sun. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will remain hard, icy and stable. There will be deposits of windslab on North-West through North to East aspects above 1100 metres. However, these areas will be shallow, limited in size, and generally avoidable.  The avalanche hazard will be Low

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It will remain dry but with strengthening Southerly winds. The freezing level will rise to around 1000 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: After a clear frosty night it was a sunny day with the freezing level rising to about 800 metres during the day. There was a breeze from the South-West.
ObservedSnowStability: The old and previously wet snowpack has frozen and consolidated. It is hard, icy and stable throughout. There was a small amount of soft fresh snow, with some deposits of windslab on North to East aspects above 1100 metres. However, these deposits were shallow and generally avoidable. The avalanche hazard is Low

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The firm snowpack will slowly soften but remain generally well bonded and stable in all locations.. Largest continuous snow areas remain on North-West through North to East aspects above around 870 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: After a cold night the freezing level will be above the summits. Partly cloudy conditions but It is expected to be dry with fresh to strong Southerly winds.
Observed Weather Influences: Colder conditions last night re-froze the moist snowpack. Today dawned with a frost at lower levels and  it was dry, bright and clear with very light summit winds, otherwise it was a calm day.
ObservedSnowStability: A light dusting of new snow arrived yesterday afternoon these accumulations were not extensive and fell mainly above 800 metres.. The older moist snowpack re-froze and is well bonded, very firm and stable. Largest continuous snow areas remain on North-West through North to East aspects above around 870 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: There will be a redistribution of lying snow with heavy drifting. Poorly bonded windslab will develop on steep wind sheltered North-West to North-East aspects, with avalanches likely above 900 metres.  West and East aspects will be affected by cross loading. Below 900 metres localised unstable windslab will develop on steep banks and burn lines. New cornices will be fragile. Elsewhere the snowpack will be firm and wind scoured. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Dry conditions expected throughout the forecast period. Strong South-South-Westerly winds will increase to gale force during the night and continue for the rest of the forecast period. During the early morning the 600 metre freezing level will rise to around 1000 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: After overnight snowfall it was a cold sunny day with light West-South-Westerly winds. The freezing level was around 600 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: There is a blanket of soft snow on all aspects, this overlies a very firm, icy snowpack. Accumulations of unstable windslab were observed on East to South aspects above 950 metres. The greatest instability is around coire rims gully tops and steep convex terrain. A small skier triggered avalanche was observed on an Easterly aspect at 1090 metres.  Elsewhere stability is generally good. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.
